title: Typed Tool Contracts, JSON Schema, Descriptions, Domains, and Versions · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-capability typed tool catalog and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Define one narrow tool purpose, effect class, caller, user benefit, authority, timeout, idempotency, and prohibited use.
Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.
Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version tool contracts and provide compatibility, migration, deprecation, and replay tests for changed parameters and results.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Tool Schema Version
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-tool-schema-version-1ls845" that uses case 7952 to design narrow typed tool contracts with purpose, descriptions, domains, required fields, rejection, and version identity. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-tool-schema-version-1ls845
# Competency: agentpy-tool-schema-version.
# Case 7952: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def evidence_agentpy_tool_schema_version_1ls845(tool_schemas: int = 8, valid_schemas: int = 8, ambiguous_parameters: int = 0, bounded_enums: int = 6, schema_versions: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ls845 = "1ls845-548sl1-7952"
    assert evidence_variant_1ls845
    evidence_axes_1ls845 = (
        "authority1ls845",
        "interaction7952",
        "tool548sl1",
        "state1ls8457952",
        "evaluation79521ls845",
        "recovery548sl17952",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ls845)) == 6
    if tool_schemas < 3 or valid_schemas < 0:
        return False, valid_schemas, "tool-schema-count"
    if valid_schemas != tool_schemas:
        return False, valid_schemas, "tool-schema-invalid"
    if ambiguous_parameters > 0 or bounded_enums < 2:
        return False, ambiguous_parameters, "parameter-ambiguity"
    if schema_versions < 2:
        return False, schema_versions, "tool-schema-version"
    assert valid_schemas == tool_schemas and ambiguous_parameters == 0
    return True, valid_schemas, "typed-tool-catalog"
